node_2: relative poses between adjacent depth maps
edge: Relative poses between adjacent depth maps can be easily injected into the training pipeline via Chamfer Distance due to monocular depth estimation.
Monocular depth estimation does not require camera parameters as input, in contrast to multi-view stereo depth estimation.
The term mono-depth is used for simplicity in reference to monocular depth estimation.
Mono-depth maps are not multi-view consistent; however, they are transformed to undistorted multiview consistent depth maps by explicitly optimizing scale and shift parameters during NeRF training.
Mono-depth maps are taken into a depth-wise loss along with NeRF training in previous works.
Scale and shift parameters for each mono-depth map are explicitly optimized during NeRF training by penalizing the difference between rendered depth and mono-depth.
NeRF is trained based on multiview consistency, which transforms mono-depth maps into undistorted multiview consistent depth maps.

edge: Multiview consistent depth maps are leveraged in two loss terms: a) a Chamfer Distance loss between two depth maps of adjacent images.
